expectations.text: Uses TaskEither (TE.TaskEither or TE.tryCatch) for async operations
expectations.evidence: Lines 63, 80, 97: all three service calls use TE.tryCatch returning TE.TaskEither<CheckoutError, T>

expectations.text: Uses pipe() to compose the steps sequentially
expectations.evidence: Line 125: `pipe(TE.Do, TE.bind(...), TE.bind(...), ...)` — entire checkout pipeline composed with pipe

expectations.text: Uses TE.chain or Do notation (TE.bind) to sequence dependent steps
expectations.evidence: Lines 129-155: uses TE.Do + TE.bind throughout for all sequential steps with named accumulator

expectations.text: Uses TE.filterOrElse or similar to validate the non-empty cart
Defines a typed error type (discriminated union or similar) rather than generic Error
expectations.evidence: Lines 44-50: `CheckoutError` discriminated union with 5 code variants (USER_NOT_FOUND, CART_NOT_FOUND, EMPTY_CART, PAYMENT_FAILED, NETWORK_ERROR)

expectations.text: Uses TE.fold or similar to handle the final result
expectations.evidence: Lines 171-186: `TE.fold((err) => { switch(err.code) ... }, (receipt) => ...)` at the HTTP handler boundary
